reasoning: As a 64-player heads-up elimination tournament, if all players were evenly matched, a single player's chance of winning would be 1/64, or ~1.56%. Even accounting for skill differences, due to the high variance in heads-up poker matches, the best players' probabilities rarely exceed 3-4%. Kristen Foxen is an elite player, but predicting anyone as the outright winner is a longshot. Therefore, her probability of winning is roughly equivalent to the base rate for top contenders, slightly rounded to 2%.
